item.title: What Does Lena Dunham Want to Tell Us?
item.description: Her new memoir captures the cost of being an impossibly popular target.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Gilbert

item.title: The Strange Origin of AI’s ‘Reasoning’ Abilities
item.description: It involves 4chan, of all places.
item.dc:creator: Alex Reisner

item.title: The 10 Things the Bezoses Are Almost Certainly Grateful for Each Morning
item.description: Never mind. We said “money” yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: The Eighth Deadly Sin
item.description: Humankind has devised a new form of debasement.
item.dc:creator: James Parker

item.title: Don’t Just Replace Chavez—Rethink Monuments
item.description: It’s time for tributes to leave the great-man theory of history behind.
item.dc:creator: Carolina A. Miranda

item.title: The Challenge the Artemis II Crew Gave the Rest of Us
item.description: Hold on to “moon joy.”
item.dc:creator: Lynn Sherr

item.title: Trump Ditched Hearts and Minds in the Iran War
item.description: The military may need to cure its counterinsurgent “hangover,” but the president has the wrong solution.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan

item.title: I Found It: The Best Free Restaurant Bread in America
item.description: Thirteen thousand miles. Infinite contenders. One beautiful loaf.
item.dc:creator: Caity Weaver

item.title: Hegseth’s Unholy War
item.description: The defense secretary seems less interested in being on the side of God than on insisting that God is on his side.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wehner

item.title: The U.S. Is Back to Squeezing Iran’s Economy
item.description: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz marks a shift in strategy—but not necessarily in outcome.
item.dc:creator: Nancy A. Youssef

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Japanese Cuisine
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Parable of the President
item.description: Donald Trump’s criticism of Pope Leo XIV reveals that to him, religion is primarily about power, not morality.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: There’s a Message for MAGA in Viktor Orbán’s Defeat
item.description: Trump and Vance chose to go all in on Hungary, and now they share in the loss.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: Photos: Hungarians Cheer Orbán’s Loss
item.description: Supporters of Hungary’s Tisza party and its lead candidate, Péter Magyar, celebrated in the streets of Budapest after soundly defeating Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s Fidesz party in a general election, removing the autocratic Orbán from office after 16 years.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Three Changes Wrought by the Iran War
item.description: The conflict will affect the economy in ways that most Americans still do not understand.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Exum

item.title: The Most Important Thing Dave Chappelle Ever Did Was Walk Away
item.description: On fearlessness, the limitations of any one narrative, and what a history of Black comedy can and can’t show
item.dc:creator: Geoff Bennett

item.title: An Oligarchy of Old People
item.description: How elderly Americans amassed disproportionate wealth and power
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: How to Tax Billionaires
item.description: Step one: Abolish the estate tax. No, really.
item.dc:creator: Ray D. Madoff

item.title: In Praise of ‘Difficult’ Kids
item.description: Feisty children can be exhausting. They also possess a moral fire that deserves cultivating.
item.dc:creator: Russell Shaw

item.title: Harmeet Dhillon Is Not Wasting Any Time
item.description: She arrived at the Department of Justice with radical changes in mind. One year later, she has completely reshaped the Civil Rights Division.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic
